expectations.text: States the principle that BusinessException throws should not live inside the AppService, and that input/invariant rules belong elsewhere
expectations.evidence: Every rule it currently violates is `throw new BusinessException(...)` inside the service body — that's the Rule 7 smell. ... app services validate input via the pipeline, call the domain service for invariants, and orchestrate persistence — they do not own business rules.

expectations.text: Moves the input-format validations (Number required / max length) onto the DTO via DataAnnotations (e.g. [Required], [StringLength(32)]) or FluentValidation — i.e. routes them through ABP's validation pipeline
expectations.evidence: [Required] [StringLength(32)] public string Number ... Both flow through AbpValidationException and produce a structured 400

expectations.text: Moves the cross-aggregate uniqueness check (existing OrderNumber) into a domain-layer concept — a *Manager / domain service (or onto the entity) — not the AppService
expectations.evidence: OrderManager : DomainService ... if (await _orderRepository.AnyAsync(o => o.Number == number)) throw new BusinessException("Sales:OrderNumberAlreadyExists");

expectations.text: Moves the Customer.IsBlocked guard into either an entity-behavior method (e.g. customer.EnsureCanReceiveNewOrder()) or the OrderManager — not the AppService
expectations.evidence: public void EnsureCanReceiveNewOrder() { if (IsBlocked) throw new BusinessException("Sales:CustomerIsBlocked"); }

expectations.text: Refactored OrderAppService no longer contains throw new BusinessException(...) statements; it orchestrates (call manager, persist, map) only
expectations.evidence: no if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(...)), no Length > 32, no customer == null, no IsBlocked check, no uniqueness query, and zero throw new BusinessException(...)

expectations.text: Domain-rule violations (uniqueness check, IsBlocked guard, customer-not-found) are signalled by throwing BusinessException (or UserFriendlyException / EntityNotFoundException) from the Manager / entity / entity-behavior method — keeps ABP's exception-based error model. Does NOT replace the throws with a functional Result<T,E> / Either / discriminated-union return type, and does NOT swallow them silently
expectations.evidence: Keep ABP's exception-based error model. Don't refactor to Result<T,E> or similar — ABP wires BusinessException / AbpValidationException / EntityNotFoundException straight into the HTTP error pipeline
